subject: Databases
title: UPSERT: Insert or Update
description: A quick win: understand UPSERT: Insert or Update.

hook.text: A user signs in. If they exist, update last_login. If not, create them. UPSERT does both in one query.

buildup.text: Postgres: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. MySQL: INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE.

discovery.text: UPSERT is atomic. No race condition between checking existence and inserting or updating.

twist.text: Without UPSERT, you'd need two queries and a transaction. More code, more chances for bugs.

climax.text: UPSERT is perfect for syncing data, deduplication, and idempotent API handlers.

punchline.text: UPSERT: one query, two outcomes. Insert or update, atomically.

quiz.question: Why is UPSERT safer than separate INSERT and UPDATE queries?
options: It's atomic, preventing race conditions between checking and writing
